# Walrus Docs

## Data Security

- [Data Security](https://docs.wal.app/docs/data-security)
    Description: Walrus provides decentralized storage for application and user data. All data
        stored on Walrus is public and can be accessed by anyone.

## Examples

- [Awesome Walrus](https://docs.wal.app/docs/examples/awesome-walrus)
    Description: https://github.com/MystenLabs/awesome-walrus
- [Checkpoint Data](https://docs.wal.app/docs/examples/checkpoint-data)
    Description: The Sui Archival application demonstrates how Sui blockchain checkpoint data can
        be archived on Walrus in a reliable, deterministic, and resilient manner. The application
        is accessible at https://walrus-sui-archival.wal.app/ The application's code is open source
        and available on GitHub.
- [Data Marketplace](https://docs.wal.app/docs/examples/data-marketplace)
- [Javascript](https://docs.wal.app/docs/examples/javascript)
    Description: The following JavaScript example shows how to upload and download a blob through a
        web form using the HTTP API.
- [Move](https://docs.wal.app/docs/examples/move)
    Description: The following Move example showcases how to import and use Walrus on-chain objects.
- [Python](https://docs.wal.app/docs/examples/python)
    Description: You can interact with Walrus from within Python code. Use the HTTP API to store
        and read blobs. Use the JSON API to store, read, and check the availability of a blob.
- [Walrus Relay](https://docs.wal.app/docs/examples/walrus-relay)
    Description: Using the Walrus TypeScript SDK and the Walrus Upload Relay, the following example
        app creates a web application that can be used for uploading and managing blobs on Walrus.
        View a deployed instance of this example in your browser or view the entire source code.

## Getting Started

- [Run a first-time install using the latest Mainnet
    version.](https://docs.wal.app/docs/getting-started/advanced-setup)
    Description: This page covers advanced setup options for Walrus, including building from
        source, installing from binaries, or using Cargo. For standard setup instructions, see the
        Walrus Getting Started guide. Walrus is open source under an Apache 2 license.
- [Index](https://docs.wal.app/docs/getting-started)
    Description: Walrus is a platform for building efficient and resilient data markets, where data
        is stored as blobs. Walrus stores each blob as an immutable array of bytes, and you can
        store any type of file, such as text, video, or source code.

## Glossary

- [Glossary](https://docs.wal.app/docs/glossary)

## Http Api

- [Managing Blobs](https://docs.wal.app/docs/http-api/managing-blobs)
- [Store 2 files `document.pdf` and `image.png`, with custom identifiers `contract-v2` and
    `logo-2024`, respectively:](https://docs.wal.app/docs/http-api/quilt-http-apis)
    Description: Walrus supports storing and retrieving multiple blobs as a single unit called a
        quilt. Publishers and aggregators both support quilt operations. All query parameters
        available for storing regular blobs can also be used when storing quilts.
- [Reading Blobs](https://docs.wal.app/docs/http-api/reading-blobs)
    Description: You can read blobs using HTTP GET requests and their blob ID or object ID.
- [Store the string `some string` for 1 storage
    epoch](https://docs.wal.app/docs/http-api/storing-blobs)
    Description: You can store data using HTTP PUT requests. For example, with cURL, you can store
        blobs using a publisher: Control how the new blob is created through a combination of
        several query parameters as documented in the OpenAPI specification.

## Legal

- [Privacy](https://docs.wal.app/docs/legal/privacy)
    Description: Last Updated: February 10, 2024 This Privacy Policy is designed to help you
        understand how Walrus Foundation (collectively called the “Organization", "we," "us," and
        "our") collects, uses, and shares your personal information and to help you understand and
        exercise your privacy rights in accordance
- [Testnet Tos](https://docs.wal.app/docs/legal/testnet_tos)
    Description: Last updated: March 26, 2025 By using Walrus Testnet software, technologies,
        tools, and other services (collectively “Testnet”) offered by Walrus Foundation ("Walrus
        Foundation", "we", "our"), you agree to the general Walrus Terms of Service and these
        additional Testnet Terms of Service (together,
- [Walrus General Tos](https://docs.wal.app/docs/legal/walrus_general_tos)
    Description: Please read these Terms of Service (the “Terms”) and our Privacy Policy carefully
        because they govern your use of the website, the user interface, and other offerings
        located at walrus.xyz, (the “Site”) offered by Walrus Foundation (“Walrus Foundation,”
        “we,” “our”).

## Operator Guide

- [Copyright (c) Walrus Foundation](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/aggregator)
    Description: Run a Walrus aggregator or publisher to expose the HTTP API. Run a local Walrus
        daemon through the binary using one of three commands: : Starts an aggregator that offers
        an HTTP interface to read blobs from Walrus. : Starts a publisher that offers an HTTP
        interface to store blobs in Walrus.
- [Auth Publisher](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/auth-publisher)
    Description: The authenticated publisher requires HTTP requests to store a blob to be
        authenticated. Use an authenticated publisher as a building block for services that require
        storing over HTTP on Walrus Mainnet, where an open publisher is undesirable because of the
        SUI and WAL cost of publishing to Walrus.
- [Basic backup command](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/backup-restore-guide)
    Description: Walrus storage nodes provide backup and restore functionality for the primary
        database containing blob data. This guide covers configuration requirements, operational
        procedures, and best practices for both automated and manual backup processes, and restore
        operations.
- [Commission Governance](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/commission-governance)
    Description: While most node parameters can be changed using and are automatically updated
        based on the values in the node configuration, authorization for contract upgrades and
        withdrawing the storage node's commission is handled separately to ensure that the hot
        wallet on the storage node does not need to be
- [Operator Guide](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/operator-guide)
    Description: This guide contains information about operating services in the network,
        specifically storage nodes, aggregators and publishers.
- [Download the script](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/signed-binaries)
    Description: Walrus release binaries and Docker images are signed using Cosign with a GCP KMS
        key. Each binary has a corresponding file containing its signature, and Docker image
        signatures are stored in the registry.
- [Storage Node Faq](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/storage-node-faq)
    Description: The storage node must have a wallet compatible with the Sui Rust SDK. This cannot
        be a hardware wallet or anything that requires user interaction for transactions, because
        the node needs to send transactions as part of normal operation. The command creates such a
        wallet.
- [Storage Node Maintenance](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/storage-node-maintenance)
    Description: This page covers the ongoing operation of your Walrus storage node after initial
        setup. Back up the directory. For database backups, see the Backup and Restore guide. The
        following metrics are the most important for monitoring node health. Set up alerts based on
        the severity levels below.
- [Storage Node Migration](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/storage-node-migration)
    Description: This guide covers migrating a Walrus storage node to new hardware. It assumes you
        run the node (and optionally an aggregator and publisher) on the same host.
- [Verify: the external IP should match where the DNS name
    resolves](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/storage-node-setup)
    Description: This page walks you through the full initial setup of a Walrus storage node, from
        system preparation to registration and first startup. :::warning If you deviate from the
        standard setup below (user, directories, ports), make sure to adjust later steps
        accordingly.
- [Storage Node](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/storage-node)
    Description: This section covers everything you need to set up and operate a Walrus storage
        node.
- [Upload Relay](https://docs.wal.app/docs/operator-guide/upload-relay)
    Description: Walrus enables dApps to store data from within their end-users' browsers having
        low to moderate machine specifications (mobile devices, low-powered laptops, and so on).

## Sites

- [Github Actions Workflow](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/ci-cd/github-actions-workflow)
    Description: You can create GitHub workflows to automate your Walrus Site deployments using the
        official Walrus Site GitHub Action. The Walrus Site action operates on an already built
        site directory. The action does not build your site, it only deploys existing static files
        to Walrus.
- [Other Ci Cd Platforms](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/ci-cd/other-ci-cd-platforms)
- [Preparing Deployment
    Credentials](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/ci-cd/preparing-deployment-credentials)
    Description: To allow a GitHub Action to deploy your Walrus Site, it needs to be able to sign
        transactions on your behalf. This requires securely providing it with your private key and
        the corresponding public address. You will need to: Export a private key from your Sui
        Wallet or CLI.
- [Adding Metadata](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/configuration/adding-metadata)
    Description: It's possible to make your Site object prettier by displaying information about it
        on Sui explorers and wallets by using the field. This is useful for adding human-readable
        information about the site. For example, you can add a link to your site's homepage, an
        image of your site's logo, and so on.
- [Setting Up Routing Rules](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/configuration/setting-up-routing-rules)
    Description: In its base configuration, Walrus Sites serves static assets through a portal.
        However, many modern web applications require more advanced features, such as custom
        headers and client-side routing.
- [Specifying Http Headers](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/configuration/specifying-http-headers)
    Description: The section allows you to specify custom HTTP response headers for specific
        resources. The keys in the object are the paths of the resources, and the values are lists
        of key-value pairs corresponding to the headers that the portal attaches to the response.
- [Bringing Your Own
    Domain](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/custom-domains/bringing-your-own-domain)
    Description: There might be cases where you want to host a Walrus Site under a specific domain
        without following the default naming convention of . For example, you might want to use a
        domain like instead of , where is a classic DNS domain that you can purchase from any
        domain registrar.
- [Dns Configuration](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/custom-domains/dns-configuration)
- [Setting A Suins Name](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/custom-domains/setting-a-suins-name)
    Description: Browsing sites with Base36 subdomains, for example, , is no longer possible using
        the portal. Base36 subdomains are still supported if you use a local server or an
        alternative portal. To browse walrus sites using the portal, you must use SuiNS names
        instead.
- [Configuration File
    Reference](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/getting-started/configuration-file-reference)
    Description: The tool requires a configuration file to know which package to use on Sui, which
        wallet to use, the gas budget, and other operational details. Most of these are abstracted
        away through sensible defaults, so you should not need to touch them.
- [Installing The Site
    Builder](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/getting-started/installing-the-site-builder)
    Description: This section describes the steps necessary to set up the Walrus Sites' tool and
        prepare your environment for development. [x] Install a recent version of Rust. [x] Follow
        all Walrus setup instructions.
- [Publishing Your First
    Site](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/getting-started/publishing-your-first-site)
    Description: The works by uploading a directory of files produced by any web framework to
        Walrus and adding the relevant metadata to Sui. This directory should have a file called in
        its root, which is the entry point to the Walrus Site.
- [Updating An Existing
    Site](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/getting-started/updating-an-existing-site)
- [Using The Site Builder](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/getting-started/using-the-site-builder)
    Description: This section describes in more detail the commands available through the . Add the
        flag to any command to get further details for the whole CLI () or individual commands (for
        example, ). The command is the primary command for managing your Walrus Site.
- [Viewing And Testing Your
    Site](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/getting-started/viewing-and-testing-your-site)
- [Index](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites)
    Description: This tutorial walks you through the steps necessary to publish a Walrus Site. We
        also provide the instructions on how to add a SuiNS name to it for convenient browsing.
        Install and setup the Walrus Sites tool for development. Publish your first Walrus Site
        using the tool.
- [Components](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/introduction/components)
    Description: Walrus Sites are "web"-sites that use Sui and Walrus as their underlying
        technology. They are a prime example of how Walrus can be used to build new and exciting
        decentralized applications. Anyone can build and deploy a Walrus Site and make it
        accessible to the world!
- [Technical Overview](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/introduction/technical-overview)
    Description: Walrus Sites are enabled by Sui and Walrus. The resources of a Walrus Site (, , ,
        images, and so on) are stored on Walrus, while the main entry point to it is an object
        stored on Sui that contains the metadata for the site and points to the Walrus blob IDs.
- [Known Restrictions](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/known-restrictions)
    Description: Walrus Sites can be used to deploy almost any form of traditional static Web2
        website built for modern browsers. There are, however, a number of restrictions that a
        developer should keep in mind when creating or porting a website to Walrus Sites.
- [Avoiding Duplicate Content
    Seo](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/linking/avoiding-duplicate-content-seo)
    Description: When you deploy your own Walrus Sites portal, you might expose the same site under
        multiple hostnames: A SuiNS domain: A human-readable names registered on-chain, for example
        . Base36: Subdomains derived directly from the site object ID, for example .
- [Linking From Walrus Sites](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/linking/linking-from-walrus-sites)
- [Linking To Walrus Sites](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/linking/linking-to-walrus-sites)
- [Linking](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/linking/linking)
    Description: This feature is currently unavailable on server-side portals. If you are browsing
        a Walrus Site on https://wal.app, you are not able to use Walrus Sites links. Certain types
        of links on Walrus Sites work differently than on a regular website.
- [Redirects](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/linking/redirects)
    Description: Consider a collection of NFTs, such as the one published by
        https://flatland.wal.app. As shown there, each minted NFT has its own Walrus Site, which
        can be personalized based on the contents, for example, the color, of the NFT itself.
- [Deploy Locally](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/portals/deploy-locally)
    Description: The term portal is used to indicate any technology that is used to access and
        browse Walrus Sites. Currently, only a server-side portal is served at https://wal.app.
        https://wal.app only serves sites deployed on mainnet, linked with SuiNS names. You can run
        a portal to access Mainnet sites as well.
- [Mainnet Testnet](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/portals/mainnet-testnet)
- [Access Control Options](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/security/access-control-options)
- [Site Data Authentication](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/security/site-data-authentication)
    Description: Walrus Sites authenticate the data that is served on the client side from the
        Walrus storage, guaranteeing with various degrees of confidence depending on the setup,
        that the site data is authentic and has not been tampered with by a malicious aggregator.
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/troubleshooting)

## Stake

- [Stake](https://docs.wal.app/docs/stake)
    Description: In Walrus, anyone can delegate stake to storage nodes and, by doing so, influence
        which storage nodes get selected for the committee in future epochs and how many shards
        these nodes hold.

## System Overview

- [NOTE: walrus-service uses these IDs to detect network defaults. Changing them changes
    node](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/available-networks)
    Description: Walrus Mainnet operates a production-quality storage network using corresponding
        resources on the Sui Mainnet. The Walrus Testnet operates in conjunction with the Sui
        Testnet and is used to test new features before they graduate to Mainnet.
- [Components](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/components)
- [Core Concepts](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/core-concepts)
    Description: Data is stored on Walrus as blobs. Each blob is an immutable array of bytes. Any
        type of file, such as text, video, or source code, can be stored on Walrus. All blobs
        uploaded to Walrus are publicly available. To secure data on Walrus, consider an encryption
        service like Seal.
- [Operations](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/operations)
    Description: Blobs can be interacted with through familiar file system operations such as
        uploading, reading, downloading, and deleting. Use the following command to upload a file
        to Walrus :::danger All blobs stored in Walrus are public and discoverable by all.
- [Public Aggregators And
    Publishers](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/public-aggregators-and-publishers)
    Description: The Walrus client offers a daemon mode that runs a simple web server that provides
        HTTP interfaces you can use to store and read blobs in an aggregator or publisher role
        respectively.
- [Quilt](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/quilt)
    Description: Quilt is a batch storage feature designed to optimize the storage cost and
        efficiency of large numbers of small blobs. Before Quilt, storing small blobs (less than 10
        MB) on Walrus involved higher per-byte costs due to internal system data overhead.
- [Red Stuff](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/red-stuff)
    Description: The RedStuff encoding algorithm used in Walrus is an adaptation of the Twin-Code
        framework presented by Rashmi et al. [1].
- [Storage Costs](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/storage-costs)
    Description: Storing blobs on Walrus Mainnet incurs 2 separate costs: WAL for the storage
        operation. See WAL tokenomics and the Walrus delegated proof of stake system for more
        details. SUI for executing transactions on Sui Mainnet. See SUI tokenomics and SUI gas fee
        calculation for more details.
- [Sui Object And Blob Ids](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/sui-object-and-blob-ids)
- [View System Info](https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/view-system-info)
    Description: The Walrus system object contains metadata about available and used storage and
        the price of storage per KB in FROST. These values are determined by 2/3 agreement between
        storage nodes for each storage epoch. You can pay to purchase storage space for specified
        durations.

## Troubleshooting

- [Error Handling](https://docs.wal.app/docs/troubleshooting/error-handling)
- [Index](https://docs.wal.app/docs/troubleshooting)
    Description: :::tip Debug logging You can enable debug logging for Walrus by setting the
        environment variable . ::: Before undertaking any other steps, make sure you have the
        latest binary. If you have multiple versions in different locations, find the binary that
        is actually used with .
- [Network Errors](https://docs.wal.app/docs/troubleshooting/network-errors)

## Tusky Migration Guide

- [Tusky Migration Guide](https://docs.wal.app/docs/tusky-migration-guide)
    Description: As of December 19, 2025, the storage service Tusky is shutting down. Data stored
        on Walrus through Tusky is still safe and accessible. During Tusky's sunsetting period, you
        can continue reading your data, however you must either migrate to using Walrus directly or
        through alternative tooling.

## Typescript Sdk

- [Configuring Network
    Requests](https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/configuring-network-requests)
- [Fetch Limitations](https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/fetch-limitations)
- [Installation And Setup](https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/installation-and-setup)
- [Managing Blobs](https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/managing-blobs)
- [Reading Blobs](https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/reading-blobs)
- [Sdks](https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/sdks)
    Description: Mysten Labs has built and published a Walrus TypeScript SDK, which supports a wide
        variety of operations. See also the related examples. The Walrus core team is actively
        working on a Rust SDK for Walrus. For data security, use the TypeScript SDK for Seal.
- [Storing Blobs](https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/storing-blobs)
- [Wasm Module Loading](https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/wasm-module-loading)

- [Client Cli](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/client-cli)
- [Examples](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/examples)
- [Glossary](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/glossary)
- [Json Api](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/json-api)
- [Networks](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/networks)
- [Quilt](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/quilt)
- [Sdks](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/sdks)
- [Setup](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/setup)
- [Stake](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/stake)
- [Started](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/started)
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/troubleshooting)
- [Web Api](https://docs.wal.app/docs/usage/web-api)

## Walrus Client

- [Configuration](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-client/configuration)
    Description: Use the option to specify a custom path to the configuration location.
- [Json Mode](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-client/json-mode)
    Description: All Walrus client commands are available in JSON mode, simplifying programmatic
        access to the CLI. All the command-line flags of the original CLI command can be specified
        in JSON format.
- [Logging And Metrics](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-client/logging-and-metrics)
    Description: The CLI allows for multiple levels of logging, which can be turned on through an
        environment variable: By default level logs are enabled, but and can give a more in depth
        understanding of what a command does or how it fails.
- [Managing Blobs](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-client/managing-blobs)
    Description: Walrus blob lifetimes can be extended using the command as long as the blob is not
        expired. Both address owned blobs and shared blobs can have their lifetime extended. Shared
        blobs can be extended by anyone, but owned blobs can only be extended by their owner.
- [Reading Blobs](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-client/reading-blobs)
    Description: The status of a blob can be queried through one of the following commands: Each
        command returns output that indicates whether the specified blob is stored and its
        availability period.
- [Storing Blobs](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-client/storing-blobs)
    Description: Use the command-line interface (CLI) to interact with the Walrus client. The CLI
        is available through installing the binary. See the getting started documentation for
        prerequisites, installation, and configuration.

## Walrus Sites

- [Advanced](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/advanced)
- [Authentication](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/authentication)
- [Avoid Duplicate Content Seo](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/avoid-duplicate-content-seo)

- [Builder Config](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/builder-config)
- [Ci Cd Gh Secrets Vars](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/ci-cd-gh-secrets-vars)
- [Ci Cd Gh Workflow](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/ci-cd-gh-workflow)
- [Ci Cd](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/ci-cd)
- [Commands](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/commands)
- [Intro](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/intro)
- [Linking](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/linking)
- [Overview](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/overview)
- [Portal](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/portal)
- [Redirects](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/redirects)
- [Restrictions](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/restrictions)
- [Routing](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/routing)
- [Tutorial Install](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/tutorial-install)
- [Tutorial Publish](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/tutorial-publish)
- [Tutorial Suins](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/tutorial-suins)
- [Tutorial](https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-sites/tutorial)
